items.headline: City launches amnesty program for unlicensed businesses
items.summary: Cupertino is offering a business license amnesty program and conducting outreach to shopping centers to encourage compliance. This could affect local business owners who may have been operating without proper licenses.

items.headline: New incentive programs for retail and startups announced
items.summary: The city council approved economic development incentives targeting retail shops, small businesses, and startups. This represents a shift in how Cupertino is trying to attract and support local business growth.

items.headline: Mixed-use housing development approved at East Middlefield Road
items.summary: The City Council approved a development agreement for a mixed-use residential project at 490 East Middlefield Road on the second reading at the March 24 meeting. This project will add new housing and mixed-use space to Mountain View.

items.headline: Mountain View applies for state housing incentive funding
items.summary: The city submitted an application for Round 4 of California's Prohousing Incentive Program at the March 24 meeting. If awarded, this state funding could support local affordable housing initiatives.

items.headline: City seeking public input on how to spend HUD housing grants
items.summary: Santa Clara is taking comments on its draft plan for federal housing funds (HUD grants) for 2026-27. This determines what housing and community development projects get funded, so residents can weigh in on priorities at the March 24 meeting.

items.headline: City holding community meeting on safe parking program
items.summary: Sunnyvale is hosting a public meeting on Monday, March 23 at 5:30 p.m. to discuss the safe parking program. This appears to be a designated parking initiative, likely related to vehicle dwellers or homelessness—a significant policy area that affects housing and street conditions in the city.
